chapters.title: — Headline: Effect Size and the Heterogeneity Problem
— Headline: Practice Impact and What Tertiary Centers Can Do Now
— Pivot and Round 1: Baucom, Twin Chorionicity and Maternal Morbidity
— Round 3: Miodownik, Placental Syndromes and Long-Term Risk
— Round 4: Huynh, Vasa Previa Hospitalization Guidelines
— Close: What to Watch, What Is Still Unsettled

follow_up_links.title: Hemodynamics-guided treatment of hypertensive disorders of pregnancy: systematic review and meta-analysis

follow_up_links.title: Hospitalization for Vasa Previa: a Review of National Guidelines

key_claims.claim: Hemodynamics-guided antihypertensive management in hypertensive disorders of pregnancy reduces composite adverse maternal and perinatal outcomes compared to blood-pressure-threshold-only standard care.
Monochorionic-diamniotic and monochorionic-monoamniotic twin gestations carry substantially higher severe maternal morbidity than dichorionic twins or singleton pregnancies.
Unsupervised machine learning can identify three clinically distinct preeclampsia phenotypes that differ in severity, angiogenic profile, and delivery timing.
Pregnancy placental syndromes serve as early indicators of long-term cardiovascular, renal, and metabolic risk, warranting postpartum surveillance.
International guidelines on antenatal hospitalization for vasa previa are inconsistent and based on weak evidentiary foundations.
